rulings.Case Title: GREGORY M. SHEFFER et al VS. KAISER FOUNDATION HOSPITALS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion To Tax Costs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, June 13, 2014, Line 3, DEFENDANTS KAISER FOUNDATION HOSPITALS, KAISER FOUNDATION HEALTH PLAN INC. AND THE PERMANENTE MEDICAL GROUP, INC'S Motion To Tax Costs The motion to tax is granted. First, plaintiff should have submitted the costs issue to the arbitrator pursuant to Corona v. Amherst Partners (2003) 107 Cal.App.4th 701. Second, the parties agreed that each party would bear its own costs "regardless of the ¿outcome of the arbitration." There is no evidence that defendant or the parties repudiated the arbitration agreement. rulings.Case Title: CATHY ENWERE VS. CLAUDECI V DASILVA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Deposition Of Plaintiff And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, June 13, 2014, Line 1, DEFENDANT CLAUDECI DASILVA's Motion To Compel Deposition Of Plaintiff And Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Nils Rosenquest, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted. No opposition filed. Sanctions awarded in the amount of $843. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to nrosenquest@earthlink.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH CONNELLY VS. COLLEEN HOLLAND et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Colleen Holland'S Motion For Appointment Of Receiver
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, June 13, 2014, Line 18, Defendant Colleen Holland'S Motion For Appointment Of Receiver This matter will be heard by Judge Sing in Dept. 306 at 9:30 am. The court will not provide a court reporter for this hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Judge Sing has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is GRANTED. Ms. Holland demonstrates that she is a co-owner of the business and Mr. Connelly has been misusing his position to her detriment. A receiver is necessary to protect her property rights. See Code of Civil Procedure section 564(b)(9); Neider v. Dardi (1955) 130 Cal.App.2d 646, 649. The Court is inclined to appoint a receiver with broad powers to manage the business and restrain Mr. Connelly from taking advantage of his position as a co-owner. Further the Preliminary Injunction, granted by the Court on May 22, 2012, has failed to protect Ms. Holland's ownership interest. Each party shall suggest in writing three persons for appointment as receiver, stating the reasons, and meet and confer, prior to the hearing, to agree on the receiver if possible. A party's suggestion is without prejudice to its objection to the appointment or confirmation of a receiver. Cal. Rules of Court 3.1177. Any party who wishes to argue the motion must notify the court (415-551-3702) and opposing counsel no later than 5:00 p.m. on June 12, 2014. If the tentative ruling is not argued, it will become the order of the court. Prevailing party to prepare the order. =302/eg

rulings.Case Title: ROY BRUBAKER VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION And Ntc
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, June 13, 2014, Line 7, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Granted. No opposition filed. Defendants shifts the burden by producing evidence that Plaintiff cannot make a prima facie case of premises liability under Gov. Code § 835. (See Evidence Cited in Support of Defendant's Separate Statement of Undisputed Material Facts ("UMF") 6 - 9, Exhibits B, C.) Further, Defendant has made a prima facie showing of design immunity under Gov. Code § 830.6. The declaration of Mr. Sallaberry shows that the street design for the area where the accident allegedly occurred was approved by employees of the San Francisco Municipal Transportation Agency and that the design was reasonable because it conformed to accepted standards. (See Sallaberry Decl., 4 - 9, Exhibit A.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.

rulings.Case Title: L&A WONG FAMILY PROPERTY ASSOCIATION LLC, et al VS. HAROLD WONG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Civil Proceedings Pending Resolution Of Irs Criminal Inbestigation And Relatred Criminal Prosecution
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, June 13, 2014, Line 8, PLAINTIFF LEM WONG's Motion To Stay Civil Proceedings Pending Resolution Of Irs Criminal Investigation And Relatred Criminal Prosecution The motion is granted in part. The court stays the oral depositions of plaintiff Lem Sek Wong and defendant Harold Wong only. Plaintiff does not persuasively demonstrate that he is the subject of a criminal IRS investigation. In addition, a total stay would be overbroad because the co-plaintiff LLC does not have any 5th Amendment rights. The temporary stay issued by order dated 5/29/14 is lifted. Plaintiff is ordered to provide the supplemental responses and pay sanctions ordered in the May 22, 2014, order by June 30, 2014. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. rulings.Case Title: GOLD CLUB - S.F., LLC, A NEVADA LIMITED LIABILITY VS. PHILLIP LANE KIDWELL, AN INDIVIDUAL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, June 13, 2014, Line 4, DEFENDANT PHILLIP LANE KIDWELL'S Motion To Quash Subpoenas. Pro Tem Judge Nils Rosenquest, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted in part and denied in part. (1) Motion is granted as to the following: American Express #3 and Visa #3 and #7. The court does not find that the plaintiff's/cross-defendant's interest in obtaining general character evidence or evidence of habit is sufficient to overcome privacy interest in unrelated credit card charges over a five year period. In lieu of a protective order, the documents should be produced with account numbers redacted to include last four digits only. Further no credit card charges by Telisa Kidwell should be included or produced, because the subpoena is directed to charges by Phillip Kidwell, Jr. only. (2) The motion is denied as to quashing the subpoenas entirely and remaining categories. Telisa Kidwell is not a target of the subpoena; so no consumer records notice needed to be served upon her. Based on the defenses and cross complaint, the charges by defendant Phillip Kidwell over the two day period in question are relevant and subject to production. To the extent that defendant Kidwell has a privacy interest in those credit card charges, the privacy is waived as to the two-day period at issue in the case. (3) Request for sanctions is denied. Court finds substantial justification for the requests and objections. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to nrosenquest@earthlink.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
